rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO ELKS LODGE NO. 3 VS. BENEVOLENT AND PROTECTIVE ORDER OF ELKS OF THE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , And In The Alternative Summary Adjudication, By Cross-Defendants San Francisco Elks No. 3 Building Association, Gregory Rougeau, Daniel Fitzpatrick, Todd Moreno, Guilluame Gassuan, John Seeley, Deborah Brown, Conor Nugent, Fred Fontana, Chantelle Parris, William Duffy, Michael Day, Kevin Hunsanger And Susan Beard
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 11, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO ELKS NO. 3 BUILDING ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , And In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burned and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact regarding present actual controversy. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANGELA LEUNG VS. STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Stonestown Shopping Center, Lp For An Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of John P. Byrne
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 11, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LP'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs is continued to July 9, 2021 for parties to submit supplemental briefs no later than June 23, 2021: (1) Defendant to submit a supplemental brief limited to citations (no further argument) to cases involving similarly worded attorney's fees clause as the one appearing in paragraph 20.2. of the Lease (as opposed to the broader clause covering "any action" in Hsu v. Abarra (1995) 9 Cal.4t" 863 cited in the moving papers; (2) Plaintiff to submit a supplemental brief, not to exceed 3 pages addressing invoices attached to reply declaration. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
